meta.schema_note: Every entry in 'services' is one card: Service Name, Category, Department, Description, Eligibility, Required Documents, Fee, Processing Time, Application Method, Office Location, Step-by-Step Procedure, FAQs, Official Website, Official PDF, Important Notes, Last Updated, Keywords — plus a 'data_confidence' field explaining how each entry was sourced.

data_confidence_levels.verified_primary_source: Fetched directly from the department's own official website in this session.
data_confidence_levels.corroborated_secondary_sources: The department's own site blocked automated access (bot detection) or had no public fee page; figures shown are what multiple independent sources agree on, but were NOT confirmed against the primary source directly. Verify before production use.

services.department: Directorate General of Immigration & Passports (DGIP), Ministry of Interior, Government of Pakistan
services.description: First-time issuance of a Pakistani passport (Machine Readable Passport or e-Passport) to a citizen who has never held one before.
services.eligibility: Any Pakistani citizen holding a valid CNIC or NICOP. Minors apply through a parent or court-appointed legal guardian.
required_documents: Proof of fee payment (bank challan receipt or e-payment confirmation with PSID)
Original valid CNIC/NICOP + photocopy
NOC from employer (government/semi-government/autonomous body staff only)
Foreign passport + photocopy (dual nationals only)
For minors: CRC/Juvenile Card (age 10+) or CRC/Form-B/FRC (under 10), plus both parents' original CNICs
services.application_method: Both — fee payment and data entry via onlinemrp.dgip.gov.pk or Passport Fee Asaan app, but in-person visit required for photograph and biometrics.
services.office_location: Any RPO/EPO/PPC or Pakistani Mission abroad. Locator: https://dgip.gov.pk/contactus/offices.php
step_by_step_procedure: Generate a PSID fee challan via Passport Fee Asaan app/web portal.
Pay via 1-Link bank/ATM/Internet banking/JazzCash/Easypaisa.
Visit an RPO/EPO/Mission with documents and paid challan.
Token issuance, photograph, and biometric capture.
Data entry and record verification.
Interview and decision by Assistant Director.
Collect passport or receive it by courier.

faqs.q: What is the fee for a normal 36-page, 5-year passport?
How long does a normal application take?

services.important_notes: Fees last officially revised 07-03-2024 (MRP/e-Passport) and 08-05-2024 (Fast Track).

services.department: Directorate General of Immigration & Passports (DGIP), Ministry of Interior, Government of Pakistan
services.description: Issuing a fresh passport to replace an existing one, before or after its expiry.
services.eligibility: Any current passport holder — no restriction on renewing before expiry.
required_documents: Same as New Passport
Original old passport (returned after action is recorded)
services.fee: Identical to New Passport fee table for the chosen page count/validity/speed.
services.processing_time: Same tiers as New Passport (21 / 5 / 2 working days).
services.application_method: Both — inland and overseas Pakistanis can apply online at onlinemrp.dgip.gov.pk; in-person visit still required for biometrics.
services.office_location: Any RPO/EPO in Pakistan, or the nearest Pakistani Mission for overseas Pakistanis.
step_by_step_procedure: Inland: apply via onlinemrp.dgip.gov.pk or visit any RPO.
Overseas: apply via onlinemrp.dgip.gov.pk or the nearest Mission.
Submit old passport; returned once action is recorded.

faqs.a: Yes, no restriction under the Passport & Visa Manual 2006.

services.important_notes: Name/DOB/father's name changes during renewal are a separate 'Modification' case routed to DGIP HQ.

services.department: Directorate General of Immigration & Passports (DGIP), Ministry of Interior, Government of Pakistan
services.description: Replacement of a passport reported lost, with an escalating fee penalty based on how many times it has been lost.
services.eligibility: The registered holder of the lost passport.
required_documents: Same as New Passport
Police report (FIR) mentioning the previous passport number
services.fee: 1st loss: 2x normal fee. 2nd loss: 4x normal fee. 3rd loss (abroad): referred to DGIP HQ for decision.
services.processing_time: Same tiers as New Passport, once the loss case is cleared.
services.application_method: Both online (fee/data entry) and in-person (police report, biometrics).
services.office_location: Concerned police station (FIR) plus any RPO/EPO/Mission.
step_by_step_procedure: Lodge FIR stating previous passport number.
Deposit the penalty fee.
Apply at RPO/EPO/Mission with police report and standard documents.
3rd-time loss abroad: Mission forwards to DGIP HQ.

faqs.q: What if I'm abroad with no passport?
faqs.a: The Mission can issue a one-way Emergency Travel Document (ETD).

services.important_notes: 3rd-time loss abroad is evaluated by DGIP HQ, not auto-approved.

services.department: Directorate General of Immigration & Passports (DGIP), Ministry of Interior, Government of Pakistan
services.description: Replacement of a physically damaged passport, or one with fully used (exhausted) pages.
services.eligibility: Any current passport holder whose passport is damaged or has no blank pages left.
required_documents: Same as New Passport
The damaged passport itself
services.fee: Standard fee for the newly chosen page count/validity — no separate damage penalty.
services.application_method: Both — fee payment online, in-person handover of damaged passport and biometrics.
step_by_step_procedure: Deposit the prescribed fee.
Visit RPO/EPO/Mission with the damaged passport and standard documents.
Choose a higher page count if pages were exhausted.

faqs.q: Is there a penalty like the lost-passport one?
faqs.a: No — standard fee for the page count/validity chosen.

services.important_notes: Exhausted-pages replacement follows the same process as damaged passport.

services.department: Directorate General of Immigration & Passports (DGIP), Ministry of Interior, Government of Pakistan
services.description: Not a separate passport product — the 'Urgent' processing-speed tier, selectable for New/Renewal/Lost/Damaged applications.
services.eligibility: Any applicant, by choosing the Urgent fee tier.
services.application_method: Both, same as the underlying service.
services.step_by_step_procedure: Same steps as the underlying service, selecting Urgent on the fee challan.

faqs.q: Is Urgent faster than Fast Track?
faqs.a: No — Fast Track (2 days, 47 cities only) is faster.

services.important_notes: Consider modeling this as a 'speed' attribute on other services rather than a standalone one.

services.description: Not a distinct passport type — a premium one-window service channel (Executive Passport Office/EPO) since 27 Oct 2015.
services.eligibility: Any applicant choosing to apply at an EPO instead of a regular RPO.
services.fee: Standard DGIP fee PLUS an additional NADRA facilitation charge at the EPO counter (varies by office, ~Rs. 3,000 commonly cited for DHA Lahore).
services.processing_time: Same DGIP tiers apply; EPO front-desk process itself ~15-20 minutes.
services.office_location: EPOs in Islamabad, Lahore (DHA), Karachi, Faisalabad, Sargodha, Sahiwal, Multan, Peshawar, Quetta, and others. List: https://dgip.gov.pk/downloads/EPO-List.pdf
step_by_step_procedure: Visit the EPO instead of a regular RPO.
Pay standard DGIP fee plus EPO service charge.
Complete photo, biometrics, data entry (~15-20 min).

faqs.a: No — same passport, faster/premium counter, extra service fee.

services.important_notes: Model as a 'service location' choice, not a passport category.

services.department: Directorate General of Immigration & Passports (DGIP), Ministry of Interior, Government of Pakistan
services.description: Passport issuance for a minor (under 18 years).
services.eligibility: Any Pakistani minor, accompanied in person by one parent or a court-appointed legal guardian.
required_documents: Age 10+: original updated CRC with fingerprints & photo, OR Juvenile Card
Under 10: original CRC/Form-B OR FRC
Original valid CNICs (or passports) of BOTH parents
Custody papers or other parent's consent if divorced/separated
Court guardianship certificate if applicable
Death certificate if a parent is deceased
Police report if replacing a lost passport
services.fee: Same MRP/e-Passport fee table as adults — no discounted child rate published.
services.application_method: Office only — parent/guardian and minor must appear in person.
step_by_step_procedure: Gather minor's ID document and both parents' CNICs.
Deposit the applicable fee.
Attend in person with the minor and accompanying parent/guardian.
Complete biometrics/photograph and data entry for the minor.

faqs.q: Can a child get a 10-year passport?
faqs.a: No — under-15s can only get 5-year validity.

services.important_notes: References NADRA's minor-ID policy (CRC/Juvenile Card split at age 10) and Passport Rules 2021.

services.department: Directorate General of Immigration & Passports (DGIP), Ministry of Interior, Government of Pakistan
services.description: Checking the status of a submitted passport application or its fee payment.
services.eligibility: Any applicant who has submitted a passport application.
services.required_documents: None — registered mobile number/email, or PSID for fee-status checks.
step_by_step_procedure: Provide mobile number/email at Data Entry stage.
Receive automatic SMS/email from shortcode 9988 ('Rabta Service') as the application progresses.
Or use the Passport Fee Asaan app/portal, or call the Help Line.

faqs.q: How will I know when my passport is dispatched?
faqs.a: Automatic SMS/email when it leaves the production facility.

services.important_notes: 9988 is DGIP's official SMS sender ID — treat any other number as suspicious.

services.department: National Database and Registration Authority (NADRA), Ministry of Interior, Government of Pakistan
services.description: First-time issuance of a Computerized/Smart National Identity Card to a Pakistani citizen who has turned 18.
services.eligibility: Pakistani citizen aged 18+, generally transitioning from a Juvenile Card/CRC record.
required_documents: Original CRC/B-Form or Juvenile Card (if previously registered as a minor)
Parents' original CNICs (for family/lineage verification)
Proof of residence (utility bill, rent agreement, etc.)
Recent passport-size photograph
In-person biometric (fingerprint) capture at a NADRA Registration Centre

fee.note: This 750/1500/2500 tier is the figure most consistently reported across multiple sources; not independently confirmed against nadra.gov.pk directly (see data_confidence).

services.application_method: Mostly Office — first-time issuance requires in-person biometric capture at an NRC; fee can be paid online.
services.office_location: Any NADRA Registration Centre (NRC). Locator: https://www.nadra.gov.pk
step_by_step_procedure: Gather required documents.
Visit an NRC and obtain a token.
Biometric verification (fingerprints, photograph).
Data entry and family-record verification.
Pay the applicable fee.
Collect the CNIC from the same NRC after the processing period.

faqs.q: Do I need to visit in person for a first CNIC?
faqs.a: Yes — biometric capture requires a physical NRC visit.

services.important_notes: nadra.gov.pk blocks automated access, so exact current fee/timeline could not be confirmed directly in this session — cross-check via NADRA helpline 0800-11786 or in person before using in production.

services.department: National Database and Registration Authority (NADRA), Ministry of Interior, Government of Pakistan
services.description: Chip-based version of the CNIC with enhanced biometric/security data storage, now NADRA's standard issuance format — the older laminated CNIC is being phased out but remains valid until its printed expiry.
services.eligibility: Same as New CNIC / Renewal — any eligible citizen applying fresh or upgrading from an old-format CNIC.
services.required_documents: Same as New CNIC; upgrading from an old card additionally requires the old CNIC.

fee.reported_range: Sources conflict: some report the same 750/1500/2500 tier as regular CNIC; others report 1,000/2,000/3,000 for Normal/Urgent/Executive specifically for Smart CNIC.
fee.note: Treat as unconfirmed until checked against nadra.gov.pk or the Pak-ID app fee calculator at the time of application.

services.processing_time: Same general tiers as New CNIC (~30 / ~15-20 / ~7-10 working days) — exact days vary slightly by source.
services.application_method: Both — renewal/upgrade can often be started online via the Pak-Identity portal (id.nadra.gov.pk) or Pak-ID app if biometric data is already on file; first-time issuance needs an NRC visit.
services.office_location: Any NADRA Registration Centre (NRC), or online via id.nadra.gov.pk for eligible renewals.
step_by_step_procedure: Check eligibility for online renewal (existing biometric data on file, no detail changes) via id.nadra.gov.pk / Pak-ID app.
If eligible: upload photo, select processing speed, pay online, receive card by courier.
If not eligible (first-time, or biometric re-capture needed): visit an NRC in person.

faqs.q: Is Smart CNIC the same thing as CNIC now?
faqs.a: For new issuance, largely yes — NADRA issues Smart CNICs by default; old-format cards already issued remain valid until they expire.

services.important_notes: Fee figures for this specific service conflict across sources more than any other NADRA service checked — verify directly before use.

services.department: National Database and Registration Authority (NADRA), Ministry of Interior, Government of Pakistan
services.description: Reissuing a CNIC/Smart CNIC that is nearing or past its expiry date, with no change to underlying data.
services.eligibility: Any CNIC holder whose card is expiring or expired.
required_documents: Copy of the expiring/expired CNIC
Proof of current residence (if changed)
Recent photograph
services.application_method: Both — online via id.nadra.gov.pk/Pak-ID app if data is unchanged and biometrics are already on file; in-person NRC visit if biometric re-capture is needed.
step_by_step_procedure: Visit id.nadra.gov.pk or the Pak-ID app.
Confirm existing data is unchanged; upload a recent photo.
Select processing speed and pay by debit/credit card.
Receive the renewed card by courier, or collect from an NRC if applying in person.

faqs.a: Yes, for most cases where data hasn't changed and no biometric re-capture is needed.

services.important_notes: Fee not independently confirmed against nadra.gov.pk directly in this session (site blocks automated fetching) — cross-check before production use.

services.department: National Database and Registration Authority (NADRA), Ministry of Interior, Government of Pakistan
services.description: Replacement card for a CNIC that has been lost, stolen, or damaged.
services.eligibility: Any CNIC holder whose card is lost, stolen, or damaged.
required_documents: Police report, if lost/stolen
Damaged card itself, if applicable
Same base documents as CNIC Renewal

fee.reported_range: Sources conflict: one cites Normal Rs. 1,200 / Urgent Rs. 2,250; another cites 'starting at Rs. 1,000' for normal.
fee.note: Higher than plain renewal in every source checked, but exact figure unconfirmed.

services.processing_time: Broadly similar tiers to CNIC Renewal, per most sources.
services.application_method: Both — online request possible in some cases, but a lost/stolen card typically needs an in-person NRC visit for verification.
step_by_step_procedure: File a police report if lost/stolen (recommended, sometimes required).
Visit an NRC or use id.nadra.gov.pk to request a duplicate.
Pay the duplicate-card fee.
Collect the new card or receive it by courier.

faqs.q: Is a duplicate CNIC more expensive than a renewal?
faqs.a: Yes, every source checked shows a higher fee than plain renewal, though the exact number is unconfirmed.

services.important_notes: Fee not independently confirmed against nadra.gov.pk directly — verify before production use.

services.department: National Database and Registration Authority (NADRA), Ministry of Interior, Government of Pakistan
services.description: A certificate listing an individual's family composition as recorded in NADRA's database — issued in three categories: By Birth (parents/siblings), By Marriage (spouse/children), or By Adoption (guardian).
services.eligibility: Any NADRA-registered citizen (CNIC/NICOP/POC holder); all family members listed must also be NADRA-registered with a 13-digit ID or CRC/B-Form.
required_documents: Applicant's CNIC/NICOP
CNIC/NICOP/POC of family members to be listed
CRC/B-Form for minors under 18, with recent photograph
Marriage certificate/Nikah Nama (for 'By Marriage' category)
Adoption Deed/Guardianship Certificate/Court Order + Affidavit of Family Verification (for 'By Adoption' category)

fee.reported_range: Sources conflict: some cite a flat Rs. 1,000 (normal and executive alike); others cite Rs. 1,000 (normal) / Rs. 2,000 (urgent/executive).
fee.note: Distinct from — and higher than — the CRC/B-Form fee below.

services.processing_time: In-person at an NRC: same day. Online via Pak-Identity: commonly cited as ~24 hours to ~7-10 working days depending on source and service tier.
services.application_method: Both — apply in person at an NRC, or online via the Pak-Identity portal (overseas Pakistanis with NICOP/POC can also apply online or via their nearest Mission).
services.office_location: Any NRC, or the nearest Pakistani Mission for overseas applicants.
step_by_step_procedure: Choose the FRC category (By Birth / By Marriage / By Adoption).
Log in to the Pak-Identity portal or visit an NRC.
Upload/submit supporting documents for each family member.
Verify the displayed family composition via a signed affidavit.
Pay the fee.
Receive the FRC same-day (in person) or by email/PDF (online).

faqs.q: Is FRC valid as a legal ID document?
faqs.a: No — it's a supporting document for visas, jobs, schools, etc., not a substitute for CNIC/Passport.

services.important_notes: If any family member isn't NADRA-registered (no CNIC/CRC), their record must be created first before an FRC can include them.

services.department: National Database and Registration Authority (NADRA), Ministry of Interior, Government of Pakistan
services.description: The first identity document for a Pakistani child under 18, linking them to their parents in NADRA's database. Also called CRC, or 'Juvenile Card' for children aged 10+.
services.eligibility: Every Pakistani citizen under 18, per Rule 18(1) of the NADRA (NIC) Rules, 2002.
required_documents: Computerized birth certificate from the Union Council/Cantonment Board (or Detailed Birth Certificate/S-1 Form for births abroad)
Both parents' original CNICs
Physical presence of the child (mandatory above age 3; for Passport-purpose CRCs, presence required even under 3)

fee.reported_figures: One source cites a notably low flat fee (~Rs. 50 normal / ~Rs. 500 executive) — much cheaper than FRC.
fee.note: Not independently confirmed; verify before use.

services.processing_time: Varies by source; not consistently reported.
services.application_method: Office — most sources state in-person application at an NRC only, with no online submission option for CRC itself (though some describe an online 'Pak Identity' flow for it, so this needs verification).
step_by_step_procedure: Obtain a computerized birth certificate from the Union Council/Cantonment Board.
Bring both parents' CNICs and the child.
Visit the nearest NRC and submit the application.
Complete verification and pay the fee.
Collect the CRC/B-Form.

faqs.q: Is B-Form the same as CRC?
faqs.a: Yes, B-Form and CRC refer to the same document; 'Juvenile Card' is the version for children aged 10+.

services.important_notes: Whether CRC can be applied for fully online is reported inconsistently across sources — treat as an in-person process by default.

services.department: National Database and Registration Authority (NADRA), Ministry of Interior, Government of Pakistan
services.description: Modifying the name recorded on a CNIC — for spelling correction, adopting a new surname after marriage/divorce, or a religion-related name change.
services.eligibility: Any CNIC holder needing to correct or change their recorded name.
required_documents: Computerized birth certificate showing the updated name, OR
NADRA affidavit — Form C1 (B-Form holders) or Form C2 (CNIC holders) — no attestation required for CNIC updates per NADRA
Dar-ul-Ifta certificate, if the change involves a religion update
Old CNIC/NICOP

fee.source_quality_note: This figure is corroborated by ProPakistani (a mainstream Pakistani tech/news outlet reporting a NADRA video guide, Aug 2025) in addition to several SEO guides — moderately higher confidence than other NADRA entries here, but still not fetched from nadra.gov.pk directly.

services.application_method: Office — biometric re-verification is required in person at an NRC.
step_by_step_procedure: Prepare the birth certificate or NADRA affidavit (Form C1/C2).
Get a Dar-ul-Ifta certificate if a religion change is involved.
Visit an NRC and obtain a token.
Submit documents; complete biometric verification.
Surrender the old CNIC.
Pay the applicable fee.
Collect the updated CNIC after processing.

faqs.q: Can I change my name more than once?
faqs.a: Reportedly, NADRA generally allows a name modification only once — this specific rule was not independently confirmed and should be verified.

services.important_notes: Moderately better-corroborated than other NADRA entries (ProPakistani + several guides agree on the 750/1500/2500 figure), but still not primary-sourced.

services.department: National Database and Registration Authority (NADRA), Ministry of Interior, Government of Pakistan
services.description: Updating the residential address recorded on a CNIC — processed as a standard 'Modification' request.
services.eligibility: Any CNIC holder who has moved or needs their address corrected.
required_documents: Proof of new address (utility bill, rent agreement, domicile certificate, etc.)
Old CNIC

fee.note: Assumed to follow the same Modification fee tier as Name Change — not separately confirmed for address changes specifically.

services.processing_time: Same tiers as Name Change (~30 / ~15 / ~7 working days).
services.application_method: Both — some address updates are reportedly startable online via id.nadra.gov.pk/Pak-ID app; others require an NRC visit.
step_by_step_procedure: Gather proof of new address.
Apply online via id.nadra.gov.pk/Pak-ID app, or visit an NRC.
Submit documents and pay the fee.
Collect the updated CNIC or receive it by courier.

faqs.q: Do I need to visit in person for an address change?
faqs.a: Not always — some address updates can be done online if no biometric re-capture is needed, but this varies.

services.important_notes: This entry is inferred from NADRA's general 'Modification' service pattern rather than a source specifically about address changes — flagged as the least-verified entry in this file.

services.department: National Database and Registration Authority (NADRA), Ministry of Interior, Government of Pakistan
services.description: Updating marital status (and, where relevant, surname) on a CNIC after marriage, or updating status after divorce.
services.eligibility: Any CNIC holder who has married or divorced.
required_documents: Nikah Nama / marriage certificate (for marriage)
Divorce decree / Talaq registration (for divorce)
Spouse's CNIC (for marriage, to enable family linkage)
Old CNIC

fee.note: Assumed to follow the same Modification fee tier — not separately confirmed for marital-status updates specifically.

services.processing_time: Same tiers as Name Change (~30 / ~15 / ~7 working days).
services.application_method: Office — biometric/family-linkage verification typically requires an NRC visit.
step_by_step_procedure: Gather Nikah Nama/marriage certificate (or divorce decree) and spouse's CNIC.
Visit an NRC and obtain a token.
Submit documents for marital-status modification.
Complete biometric verification.
Pay the fee.
Collect the updated CNIC.

faqs.q: Does this also update my Family Registration Certificate?
faqs.a: You'd typically need to separately request an updated FRC 'By Marriage' after your CNIC marital status is updated.

services.important_notes: Inferred from NADRA's general Modification service pattern rather than a source specifically about marriage updates — flagged as lower-confidence.
